article.body_preview:
Dhaka (AsiaNews) - Bangladesh is facing an energy crisis. Power cuts can last eight to 10 hours every day.

With the price of fuel to run generators rising and disruptions in energy supply in both cities and agricultural areas, doing business is a daily challenge.

For people like Bablu Ratno, a Catholic printer, outages mean higher costs and delays in delivering products to customers.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: disruptions in production will cut into exports
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article refers to disruptions across most factories and main manufacturing regions in Bangladesh, describing a country-level reduction in exports which directly affects the exports component of the national balance of trade.

verification.quote_best_match: disruptions in production will cut into exports,
verification.judge_reasoning: The article explicitly states that industry officials fear disruptions in production will cut into exports. It discusses widespread disruptions across most factories and main manufacturing regions in Bangladesh, indicating a country-level impact on exports relevant to the balance of trade.
